basics.name: Jian Sun
basics.summary: PhD applicant in Fatigue & Fracture Mechanics · M.S. from UCAS, Institute of Mechanics · First-author publications in Engineering Fracture Mechanics and International Journal of Fatigue

education.institution: University of Chinese Academy of Sciences, Institute of Mechanics

publications.name: Mechanism of artificial surface defect induced cracking for very high cycle fatigue of Ti alloys
publications.summary: First-author paper. Investigated VHCF behavior of TC17 titanium alloy with artificial surface defects, revealing synergistic effect of cyclic stress and environmental attack. Using SEM and EBSD to identify nanograin formation and twinning in α grains.

publications.name: Effects of Notches and Defects on Dwell Fatigue Mechanism and Fatigue Life of Ti-6Al-4V ELI Alloy Used in Deep-Sea Submersibles
publications.publisher: Journal of Marine Science and Engineering
publications.summary: First-author paper. Examined notch sensitivity, defect tolerance, and failure mode transitions of Ti-6Al-4V ELI alloy under dwell fatigue loading for deep-sea applications.

publications.name: A method of quasi in-situ EBSD observation for microstructure and damage evolution in fatigue and dwell fatigue of Ti alloys
publications.summary: Co-author. Developed quasi in-situ EBSD methodology to track microstructural evolution (twinning, subgrain formation) under cyclic loading. Captured refined grain formation due to dislocation sliding and twinning.

publications.name: Effects of Natural Aging and Discontinuous Cyclic Loading on High Cycle Fatigue Behavior of Steels
publications.summary: Co-author. Investigated long-term natural aging (>20,000h) effects on fatigue life of 25CrMo4 and 30CrMnSiA steels, attributing reduction to corrosion and hydrogen action.

teaching.institution: Lanzhou University of Technology, Department of Mechanics
teaching.description: Assisted undergraduate students in preparing for university-level mechanics competitions. Led team to achieve First and Third Prizes in the University Mechanics Competition.

teaching.institution: Lanzhou University of Technology, Department of Mechanics
teaching.description: Organized academic seminars and hands-on workshops. Led the association to win the 'Top Ten Campus Associations' title.

portfolio.name: Very High Cycle Fatigue of TC17 Titanium Alloy
portfolio.description: First-author publication in Engineering Fracture Mechanics. Investigated artificial surface defect induced cracking mechanism in VHCF regime using SEM/EBSD.

portfolio.name: Quasi In-Situ EBSD Methodology Development
portfolio.description: Co-authored publication in International Journal of Fatigue. Developed method to track microstructural evolution (twinning, subgrain formation) during fatigue and dwell fatigue.

portfolio.name: Dwell Fatigue of Ti-6Al-4V ELI for Deep-Sea Submersibles
portfolio.description: First-author publication in JMSE. Examined notch sensitivity, defect tolerance, and failure mode transitions in titanium alloy for deep-sea applications.
